revision.comment: 新しいページ：ツボリ山古墳（奈良県平群町にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = ツボリ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室入り口 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 47.93 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[方墳]]または[[円墳]] |規模 = 一辺（直径）20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式石棺2基） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「ツボリ山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = ツボリ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''ツボリ山古墳'''（つぼりやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴（ふき）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]または[[円墳]]。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県北西部、平群谷において[[生駒山地]]から東へ延びる尾根の南側斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。古くより石室が開口して墳丘も大きく削平を受けているほか、[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に保存整備のための調査が実施されている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は方形または円形で、一辺（直径）約20メートルを測る{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}史跡説明板。。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。主体部の埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口する。玄室・羨道には各1基の[[二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製の刳抜式石棺が据えられる。副葬品は明らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。使用例の限られる刳抜式石棺が2基並ぶ点で、被葬者の身分の高さが注目される古墳である{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}{{small|中央に玄室棺。}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては、両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html ツボリ山古墳]（平群町ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.25メートル、幅2.2-2.55メートル、高さ2.45メートル * 羨道：長さ4.65メートル、幅1.7-1.82メートル、高さ約1.7メートル 玄室は2段積みで2段目は内傾し、天井部は巨石2個で架構される{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。羨道には巨石3枚が横に並べられるが、上段・天井石は欠失する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。調査時には羨道入り口で閉塞石として人頭大の円礫が認められている。 玄室・羨道にはそれぞれ刳抜式家形石棺1基・刳抜式石棺1基が据えられている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石材はいずれも[[二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製である。各棺の詳細は次の通り。 * 玄室棺（刳抜式家形石棺） *: 玄室ほぼ中央の位置に、石室主軸と平行する方向で据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身および蓋石の3分の1を遺存する。各石の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.45メートル、幅1.17メートル、高さ0.8メートル *:** 内法：長さ1.55メートル、幅0.55メートル、深さ0.42メートル *:* 蓋石：長さ不明、幅1.19メートル、高さ0.7メートル *:** 内法：深さ0.11メートル *: 蓋石には前後（小口側）1個・左右（側面側）2個の縄掛突起を付す。 * 羨道棺（刳抜式石棺：家形石棺か） *: 玄門から羨道にかけての位置に据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身のみで蓋石を欠失し、棺身も側面を欠損する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石棺の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.35メートル、幅1.19メートル、高さ不明 *: 玄室棺に対してやや小振りであることから、追葬棺とみる説がある{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|羨道（入り口方向）
{{small|中央に羨道棺。}} File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsukan.jpg|玄室棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendoukan.jpg|羨道棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|石室入り口 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** ツボリ山古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）3月15日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1996年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}|ref=}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1972|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=烏土塚古墳（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第27冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[烏土塚古墳]] * [[西宮古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html ツボリ山古墳] - 平群町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:つほりやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡‎]]

墳丘・石室入り口 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 47.93 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[方墳]]または[[円墳]] |規模 = 一辺（直径）20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式石棺2基） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「ツボリ山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = ツボリ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''ツボリ山古墳'''（つぼりやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴（ふき）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]または[[円墳]]。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県北西部、平群谷において[[生駒山地]]から東へ延びる尾根の南側斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。古くより石室が開口して墳丘も大きく削平を受けているほか、[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に保存整備のための調査が実施されている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は方形または円形で、一辺（直径）約20メートルを測る{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}史跡説明板。。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。主体部の埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口する。玄室・羨道には各1基の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製の刳抜式石棺が据えられる。副葬品は明らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。使用例の限られる刳抜式石棺が2基並ぶ点で、被葬者の身分の高さが注目される古墳である{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}{{small|中央に玄室棺。}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては、両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html ツボリ山古墳]（平群町ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.25メートル、幅2.2-2.55メートル、高さ2.45メートル * 羨道：長さ4.65メートル、幅1.7-1.82メートル、高さ約1.7メートル 玄室は2段積みで2段目は内傾し、天井部は巨石2個で架構される{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。羨道には巨石3枚が横に並べられるが、上段・天井石は欠失する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。調査時には羨道入り口で閉塞石として人頭大の円礫が認められている。 玄室・羨道にはそれぞれ刳抜式家形石棺1基・刳抜式石棺1基が据えられている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石材はいずれも[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製である。各棺の詳細は次の通り。 * 玄室棺（刳抜式家形石棺） *: 玄室ほぼ中央の位置に、石室主軸と平行する方向で据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身および蓋石の3分の1を遺存する。各石の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.45メートル、幅1.17メートル、高さ0.8メートル *:** 内法：長さ1.55メートル、幅0.55メートル、深さ0.42メートル *:* 蓋石：長さ不明、幅1.19メートル、高さ0.7メートル *:** 内法：深さ0.11メートル *: 蓋石には前後（小口側）1個・左右（側面側）2個の縄掛突起を付す。 * 羨道棺（刳抜式石棺：家形石棺か） *: 玄門から羨道にかけての位置に据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身のみで蓋石を欠失し、棺身も側面を欠損する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石棺の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.35メートル、幅1.19メートル、高さ不明 *: 玄室棺に対してやや小振りであることから、追葬棺とみる説がある{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|羨道（入り口方向）
{{small|中央に羨道棺。}} File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsukan.jpg|玄室棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendoukan.jpg|羨道棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|石室入り口 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** ツボリ山古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）3月15日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1996年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}|ref=}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1972|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=烏土塚古墳（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第27冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[烏土塚古墳]] * [[西宮古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html 県指定 ツボリ山古墳] - 平群町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:つほりやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡‎]]

（内部に刳抜式石棺2基） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「ツボリ山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = ツボリ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''ツボリ山古墳'''（つぼりやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴（ふき）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]または[[円墳]]。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県北西部、平群谷において[[生駒山地]]から東へ延びる尾根の南側斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。古くより石室が開口して墳丘も大きく削平を受けているほか、[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に保存整備のための調査が実施されている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は方形または円形で、一辺（直径）約20メートルを測る{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}史跡説明板。。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。主体部の埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方に開口する。玄室・羨道には各1基の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製の刳抜式石棺が据えられる。副葬品は明らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。使用例の限られる刳抜式石棺が2基並ぶ点で、被葬者の身分の高さが注目される古墳である{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}{{small|中央に玄室棺。}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては、両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html ツボリ山古墳]（平群町ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.25メートル、幅2.2-2.55メートル、高さ2.45メートル * 羨道：長さ4.65メートル、幅1.7-1.82メートル、高さ約1.7メートル 玄室は2段積みで2段目は内傾し、天井部は巨石2個で架構される{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。羨道には巨石3枚が横に並べられるが、上段・天井石は欠失する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。調査時には開口部で閉塞石として人頭大の円礫が認められている。 玄室・羨道にはそれぞれ刳抜式家形石棺1基・刳抜式石棺1基が据えられている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石材はいずれも[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製である。各棺の詳細は次の通り。 * 玄室棺（刳抜式家形石棺） *: 玄室ほぼ中央の位置に、石室主軸と平行する方向で据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身および蓋石の3分の1を遺存する。各石の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.45メートル、幅1.17メートル、高さ0.8メートル *:** 内法：長さ1.55メートル、幅0.55メートル、深さ0.42メートル *:* 蓋石：長さ不明、幅1.19メートル、高さ0.7メートル *:** 内法：深さ0.11メートル *: 蓋石には前後（小口側）1個・左右（側面側）2個の縄掛突起を付す。 * 羨道棺（刳抜式石棺：家形石棺か） *: 玄門から羨道にかけての位置に据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身のみで蓋石を欠失し、棺身も側面を欠損する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石棺の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.35メートル、幅1.19メートル、高さ不明 *: 玄室棺に対してやや小振りであることから、追葬棺とみる説がある{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|羨道（開口部方向）
{{small|中央に羨道棺。}} File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsukan.jpg|玄室棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendoukan.jpg|羨道棺 File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** ツボリ山古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）3月15日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1996年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}|ref=}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1972|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=烏土塚古墳（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第27冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[烏土塚古墳]] * [[西宮古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html 県指定 ツボリ山古墳] - 平群町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:つほりやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

内法：深さ0.11メートル *: 蓋石には前後（小口側）1個・左右（側面側）2個の縄掛突起を付す。 * 羨道棺（刳抜式石棺：家形石棺か） *: 玄門から羨道にかけての位置に据えられる{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身のみで蓋石を欠失し、棺身も側面を欠損する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石棺の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.35メートル、幅1.19メートル、高さ不明 *: 玄室棺に対してやや小振りであることから、追葬棺とみる説がある{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsukan.jpg|玄室棺 Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendoukan.jpg|羨道棺 Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** ツボリ山古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）3月15日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（奈良県教育委員会、1996年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}|ref=}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1972|chapter=ツボリ山古墳|title=烏土塚古墳（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第27冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[烏土塚古墳]] * [[西宮古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html 県指定 ツボリ山古墳] - 平群町ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:つほりやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = ツボリ山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 37|緯度秒 = 47.93 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[方墳]]または[[円墳]] |規模 = 一辺（直径）20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式石棺2基） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「ツボリ山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = ツボリ山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''ツボリ山古墳'''（つぼりやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]福貴（ふき）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]または[[円墳]]。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県北西部、平群谷において[[生駒山地]]から東へ延びる尾根の南側斜面に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。古くより石室が開口して墳丘も大きく削平を受けているほか、[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に保存整備のための調査が実施されている{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は方形または円形で、一辺（直径）約20メートルを測る{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}史跡説明板。。墳丘外表で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室・羨道には各1基の[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製の刳抜式石棺を据える。副葬品は詳らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]初頭頃と推定される。使用例の限られる刳抜式石棺が2基並ぶ点で、被葬者の身分の高さが注目される古墳である{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf ふるさとへぐり再発見18「ツボリ山古墳」]}}（リンクは平群町ホームページ）。。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html ツボリ山古墳]（平群町ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.25メートル、幅2.2-2.55メートル、高さ2.45メートル * 羨道：長さ4.65メートル、幅1.7-1.82メートル、高さ約1.7メートル 玄室は2段積みで2段目は内傾し、天井部は巨石2個で架構される{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。羨道には巨石3枚を横に並べるが、上段・天井石は欠失する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。調査時には開口部で閉塞石として人頭大の円礫が認められている。 玄室・羨道にはそれぞれ刳抜式家形石棺1基・刳抜式石棺1基を据える{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石材はいずれも[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]白色[[凝灰岩]]製である。各棺の詳細は次の通り。 * 玄室棺（刳抜式家形石棺） *: 玄室ほぼ中央の位置に、石室主軸と平行する方向に据える{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身および蓋石の3分の1を遺存する。各石の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.45メートル、幅1.17メートル、高さ0.8メートル *:** 内法：長さ1.55メートル、幅0.55メートル、深さ0.42メートル *:* 蓋石：長さ不明、幅1.19メートル、高さ0.7メートル *:** 内法：深さ0.11メートル *: 蓋石には前後（小口側）1個・左右（側面側）2個の縄掛突起を付す。 * 羨道棺（刳抜式石棺：家形石棺か） *: 玄門から羨道にかけての位置に据える{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。棺身のみで蓋石を欠失し、棺身も側面を欠損する{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石棺の規模は次の通り。 *:* 棺身：長さ2.35メートル、幅1.19メートル、高さ不明 *: 玄室棺に対してやや小振りであることから、追葬棺とみる説がある{{Sfn|ツボリ山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** ツボリ山古墳 - {{Japanese burial mounds |Name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Utsuzuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{{{Infobox ancient sites |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Utsuzuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Utsuzuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient sites |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Utsuzuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] -
