(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: * Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: * Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): * Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters * Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters * The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: * Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: * Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites * Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishimiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] -
