{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Heguri Town Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture historic sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[ja:ツボリ山古墳]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Tsubori-yama Kofun|ja|ツボリ山古墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - (includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}== See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}== See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Heguri Town Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofuns in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nara Prefecture historic sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[ja:ツボリ山古墳]] * [[Nishinomiya Kofun]]''' {{ill|Tsubori-yama Kofun|ja|ツボリ山古墳}} ... 10 KB (1,251 words) - 04:31, 15 January [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Tsuboriyama Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsuboriyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tsuboriyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] | align="center"|[[Tsuboriyama Kofun]]

Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45年}} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year }} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48年}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |Alias = |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, Fuki |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 37|Latitude seconds = 47.93 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 41|Longitude seconds = 47.52 |ISO = JP-29 |Shape = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Scale = 20m (side/diameter) |Burial structure = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(includes two hollowed-out stone coffins) |Excavated items = |Accompanying tombs = |Construction period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Cultural property designation = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Nara |Icon = |Label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Heguri Town|ja|平群町}}, {{ill|Ikoma District|ja|生駒郡}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, in the Fuki area. Its shape is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and it is designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in {{ill|1970|ja|昭和45th year }} to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historical Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out stone coffins made from white {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} from {{ill|Mount Nijo|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡}} in {{ill|1973|ja|昭和48th year }}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening southward. The chamber dimensions are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out stone coffins (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. Dimensions are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == Notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historical Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Udozuka Kofun|ja|烏土塚古墳}} * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] -
