Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|qq=|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], Fuki |coordinates = {{Coord|34.629980555556|N|135.69653333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 20m (side/diameter) |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(includes two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss) |excavations = |Accompanying tombs = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = |map_dot_label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], Fuki |coordinates = {{Coord|34.629980555556|N|135.69653333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 20m (side/diameter) |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(includes two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss) |excavations = |Accompanying tombs = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = |map_dot_label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historic Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Udozuka Kofun|Udozuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historic Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Udozuka Kofun|Udozuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuboriyama Kofun]][[ja:ツボリ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65240603]] Wikidata name: Tsuboriyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], Fuki |coordinates = {{Coord|34.629980555556|N|135.69653333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 20m (side/diameter) |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(includes two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss) |excavations = |Accompanying tombs = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = |map_dot_label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], Fuki |coordinates = {{Coord|34.629980555556|N|135.69653333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 20m (side/diameter) |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(includes two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss) |excavations = |Accompanying tombs = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = |map_dot_label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tsuboriyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], Fuki |coordinates = {{Coord|34.629980555556|N|135.69653333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = 20m (side/diameter) |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(includes two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss) |excavations = |Accompanying tombs = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic Site = Designated as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} "Tsuboriyama Kofun" |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = |map_dot_label = Tsuboriyama
Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historic Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Udozuka Kofun|Udozuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historic Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Udozuka Kofun|Udozuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] - Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} '''Tsuboriyama Kofun''' (ツボリ山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri Town]], [[:en:Ikoma District, Nara|Ikoma District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], in the Fuki area. Its type is a {{ill|Square mound|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Round mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and it is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == This kofun is located on the southern slope of a ridge extending eastward from the {{ill|Ikoma Mountains|ja|生駒山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Mountains|1=Ikoma Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574917}} in northwestern Nara Prefecture, within the Heguri Valley. The stone chamber has been exposed for a long time, and the mound has undergone significant erosion. A preservation survey was conducted in [[:en:1970|1970]] to facilitate conservation efforts{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The mound is either square or circular, with an approximate side/diameter of 20 meters{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}Historic Site Interpretation Board.. The mound lacks evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility is a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward. Within the chamber, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss made from white [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] are placed. The construction date is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. The presence of two rare hollowed-out coffins highlights the social prominence of the interred individuals{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}} (Heguri Town Website).. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Nara Prefecture historic site|ja|奈良県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1973|1973]]. == burial == [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility features a two-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. The chamber area are as follows[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Tsuboriyama Kofun] (Heguri Town Website).: * Total length: 8 meters * Main chamber: ** Length: 4.25 meters, Width: 2.2–2.55 meters, Height: 2.45 meters * Corridor: ** Length: 4.65 meters, Width: 1.7–1.82 meters, Height: approx. 1.7 meters The main chamber walls consist of two tiers, with the second tier inclined inward. The ceiling comprises two large stones{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The corridor features three horizontally arranged large stones, although the upper layers and ceiling stones are missing{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. In the chamber and corridor, two hollowed-out Sarcophaguss (a house-shaped one in the chamber and a smaller one in the corridor) are placed{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}: * Chamber coffin (house-shaped): ** Located in the center of the chamber, aligned with the main axis{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The coffin is partially preserved. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.45 meters, Width 1.17 meters, Height 0.8 meters ** Lid: Length unknown, Width 1.19 meters, Height 0.7 meters ** The lid includes rope-like protrusions on its sides. * Corridor coffin: ** Smaller and likely used for secondary burial{{Sfn|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. Only the coffin body remains, with the lid missing. area are as follows: ** Coffin body: Length 2.35 meters, Width 1.19 meters, Height unknown File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards rear wall) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor (towards entrance) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards main chamber) File:Tsuboriyama Kofun, entrance-3.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Sites ** Tsuboriyama Kofun - Designated on March 15, 1973{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf List of Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site]}} (Nara Prefecture Website).. == notes == {{note help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{small|Sources used for article creation}} * Historic Site Interpretation Board (Nara Prefecture Board of Education, 1996). * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/pdf/furusatohegurisaihakken_018.pdf Rediscovering Furusato Heguri 18: Tsuboriyama Kofun]}}|ref=}} (Heguri Town Website). * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1981|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30: Nara Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Tsuboriyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == Related Documents == {{small|Sources not used for article creation}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=|year=1972|chapter=Tsuboriyama Kofun|title=Utsuzuka Kofun (Nara Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Report, Volume 27)|publisher=Nara Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Udozuka Kofun|Udozuka Kofun]] * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsuboriyama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/web/kankou/bunkazai/bunkazai04-27.html Prefectural Designation: Tsuboriyama Kofun] -
